chunks.title: video
chunks.text: next thing which we are going to study are operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we have used operators in our day to day life in math

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: but now we will use operators in programming

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: till now we have seen statement in math that sum is equal to a plus b

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: this statement has plus in it

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: this is called an operator, operator's job is to perform some operation

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: plus operator performs addition, minus operator performs subtraction

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: similarly to show multiply we use star

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so we multiply with star operator and we have many different types of operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: in fact in programming this equal to is also an operator

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: which we call assignment operator

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and we don't have only one assignment operator, we have many types of assignment operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we will see their types too, but these are basically operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and these a and b on which operation is performed, operation of sum

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we call them operands

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: operator performs operation on operands

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: this is a very complex line, but we can remember two words, the one who performs operation

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we call that symbol as operator and on whom operation is performed

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we call them as operands, so the first and easiest operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: they are called arithmetic operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: in arithmetic operators all mathematical operations are there

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: like plus, minus, multiply which will be asterisk

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: which is the star symbol, divide and with this we also have something

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: called modulo and star star, so let's see these four basic operations

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: let's clear this and first of all

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we will see in detail our arithmetic

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: operators, in arithmetic operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: let's take two numbers as example, a is equal to 10 and b is equal to 5

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so here if we want we can print a plus b

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so with this our addition operation will be performed

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: subtraction will be performed from minus b, multiplication will be performed

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and divide will be performed from a divided by b

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so here this is division and this is multiplication

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: subtraction and addition, these are very simple operations

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so let's run our code, so 10 plus 5 is 15, 10 minus 5 is 5

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: 10 into 5 is 50 and 10 divided by 5 is 2.0

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: now why 2.0 is printed instead of 2, we will discuss the reason in a while

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: but for now we can see that all the operations are working correctly

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: with this we have two more operations which are very interesting

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: first is the modulo operator

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: this operator which is the sign of our percentage is called modulo

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: modulo's job is to calculate the remainder

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: remainder means if one number is divided by another number

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: then the remainder which is left after dividing, its value is given by modulo

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: like we know that when 10 is divided by 5 then the remainder is 0

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so here if we print

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: a modulo b and here this is the modulo value

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: let's clear this

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so the last value that we are going to get is 0

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so if we had done 9, then 9 divided by 5 is 1

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: but in modulo we get 4, let's clear this and rerun

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so here we have 4 in modulo, here by pausing

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: you can also double check that all these values are consistent with these operations

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so whenever we have to get the remainder we use modulo

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: similarly whenever we have to get a to the power b

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: when we have to calculate this value then we write a asterisk asterisk b

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so from here a to the power b is calculated

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so here if i write 10 and 5

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so to calculate power i can simply print a asterisk asterisk b

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so we know that we will get 10 to the power 5 which is 1 lakh

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so let's clear this and rerun the code

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so the last value that we have got is equal to 1 lakh

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so in this way all our arithmetic operators work

similarly to show multiply we use star so we multiply with star operator and we have many different types of operators in fact in programming this equal to is also an operator which we call assignment operator and we don't have only one assignment operator, we have many types of assignment operators we will see their types too, but these are basically operators and these a and b on which operation is performed, operation of sum we call them operands operator performs operation on operands this is a very complex line, but we can remember two words, the one who performs operation we call that symbol as operator and on whom operation is performed we call them as operands, so the first and easiest operators they are called arithmetic operators in arithmetic operators all mathematical operations are there like plus, minus, multiply which will be asterisk which is the star symbol, divide and with this we also have something called modulo and star star, so let's see these four basic operations let's clear this and first of all we will see in detail our arithmetic operators, in arithmetic operators let's take two numbers as example, a is equal to 10 and b is equal to 5 so here if we want we can print a plus b so with this our addition operation will be performed subtraction will be performed from minus b, multiplication will be performed and divide will be performed from a divided by b so here this is division and this is multiplication subtraction and addition, these are very simple operations so let's run our code, so 10 plus 5 is 15, 10 minus 5 is 5 10 into 5 is 50 and 10 divided by 5 is 2.0 now why 2.0 is printed instead of 2, we will discuss the reason in a while but for now we can see that all the operations are working correctly with this we have two more operations which are very interesting first is the modulo operator this operator which is the sign of our percentage is called modulo modulo's job is to calculate the remainder remainder means if one number is divided by another number then the remainder which is left after dividing, its value is given by modulo like we know that when 10 is divided by 5 then the remainder is 0 so here if we print whenever we have to get a to the power b when we have to calculate this value then we write a asterisk asterisk b so from here a to the power b is calculated so here if i write 10 and 5 so to calculate power i can simply print a asterisk asterisk b so we know that we will get 10 to the power 5 which is 1 lakh so let's clear this and rerun the code so the last value that we have got is
